Home»Sport»Paris Olympics 2024»Team SA wins second medal at Paris Olympics 2024

Mountain biker Alan Hatherly won Team SA’s second medal of the Paris Olympics 2024 when he picked up bronze in the men’s cross-country race.

Mountain bikerAlan HatherlywonTeam SA’ssecondmedal of theParis Olympics 2024when he picked upbronzein the men’s cross-country race on an energy-sapping course on Monday.

Enjoy the Paris Olympic Games 2024 experience at your favourite ATKV Resort!

Gold was won by Britain’sTom Pidcock, with silver going to France’sVictor Koretzky.

Hatherly joins Team SA’s men’s rugby squad as bronze medallists at the Games – so far.

It was cycling’s first medal for South Africa at the Games since returning from intrenational isolation in 1992.

For the latest Paris Olympics 2024 news, bookmarkThe South African website’sdedicated section for free-to-read content

Pidcock roared back after suffering a puncture to retain his Olympic men’s mountain bike title. He found himself 35 seconds adrift after a front wheel puncture on the fourth of eight laps on the 4.4 km circuit at Elancourt Hill. Pidcock shit the front on lap seven and looked set to power ahead. But Koretzky and Hatherly stayed with him to set up a blockbuster final lap.

A mistake by the Frenchman allowed Pidcock to ride clear, crossing the line nine seconds ahead of Koretzky with some fans booing.

Hatherly had held the advantage after the end of the first lap, but he was passed and after two laps a group of seven, including Hatherley, had broken away from the field.

On the third lap Pidcock made his move to strike the front, while Hatherly had got back into four place. The South African refused to away and forced his way back into medal contention, working back into the silver medal position and looking to hunt down Koretzky who had surged his way to the lead.

Koretzky started to open up a gap on his rivals and after six laps he was 17 seconds ahead of Hatherly and Pidcock, who was rallying back after the puncture.

On the penultimate lap Pidcock piled the pressure on, and the Frenchman started to feel the heat. What Pidcock was doing was dragging the South African with him, and suddenly the three had the race to themselves. It seemed as though a medal was guaranteed for Hatherly, the only question being what colour.

By the last lap a blanket could be thrown over the leading three riders. However, it was Pidcock who had the leg reserve and he broke away late to take gold in 1:26.22.

Koretzky held on for second and Hatherly was third in 1:26.33.

title: Team SA wins second medal at Paris Olympics 2024
author: Garrin Lambley
description: Mountain biker Alan Hatherly won Team SA’s second medal of the Paris Olympics 2024 when he picked up bronze in the men’s cross-country race.
